description:
You are a web-developer who has just been handed the raw access log of an Nginx
    server and have been asked to prepare a couple of lightweight, machine-readable
    reports that can later be shipped to a data-warehouse.

    The log file is already on-disk:

        /home/user/projects/website/logs/access.log

    Your job is to extract two different summaries **only for the traffic that
    happened on 15 Aug 2023 (already the only date in the log)** and save them to
    the new directory

        /home/user/projects/website/reports

    (Create the directory if it does not yet exist.)  
    Everything you create in this task **must** live inside that directory.

    ----------------------------------------------------------------------
    1. status_per_hour.csv
    ----------------------------------------------------------------------
    • Location : /home/user/projects/website/reports/status_per_hour.csv  
    • Format   : plain UTF-8 text, comma-separated values.  
    • First line **must be the header** exactly (including case):
          hour,status_code,count
    • Subsequent lines describe the amount of responses **per status code and
      hour**.  
      – hour is the two-digit hour in server time (00-23).  
      – status_code is the 3-digit HTTP status.  
      – count is the number of log lines that match this hour/status pair.  
    • Sorting rules:  
      1. Ascending by hour (numerical).  
      2. For identical hours sort ascending by status_code (numerical).  
    • There must be **no spaces** around the commas and **no blank lines** at the
      end of the file.

    ----------------------------------------------------------------------
    2. top5_endpoints.txt
    ----------------------------------------------------------------------
    • Location : /home/user/projects/website/reports/top5_endpoints.txt  
    • Format   : plain UTF-8 text.  
    • Exactly five lines, each one shaped like (quotes not included):
          <rank>. <endpoint> <hits>
      Where  
        – <rank>    is an integer 1-5,  
        – <endpoint> is the request path exactly as it appeared in the log
          (case-sensitive, do **not** include a query string – there are none in
          this log),  
        – <hits>    is the number of times that endpoint was requested.  
    • Lines are ordered by descending <hits>.  
      If two endpoints have the same number of hits, order them
      alphabetically (byte-wise) by <endpoint>.  
    • No extra spaces or trailing blank lines.

    ----------------------------------------------------------------------
    3. Compressed bundle
    ----------------------------------------------------------------------
    Create a gzipped tarball named

        /home/user/projects/website/reports/aug15_summary.tar.gz

    that contains **both** of the above files at archive root:
        status_per_hour.csv
        top5_endpoints.txt

    ----------------------------------------------------------------------
    Verification notes
    ----------------------------------------------------------------------
    1. The automated grader will untar aug15_summary.tar.gz and compare the two
       files to reference copies byte-for-byte, so all requirements about header
       wording, ordering and whitespace are important.
    2. The original log file must remain untouched.
    3. Use only user-level permissions; do not attempt to sudo.
    4. You are free to use any standard GNU/Linux text-processing utilities
       (awk, sed, cut, grep, sort, uniq, comm, paste, tee, etc.).  Feel free to
       build the reports using pipes, redirections or temporary files, but only
       the two final artefacts and the tarball should live in
       /home/user/projects/website/reports when you are done.
